description_long: This is a small, single-engine, fixed-wing airplane with a low-wing configuration and a streamlined fuselage. The exterior is predominantly white with subtle gray and red striping along the side, and the registration number '4X-CWT' is clearly visible on the tail section. The aircraft features a tricycle landing gear setup, with two main wheels under the wings and a nose wheel beneath the cockpit. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the cockpit is enclosed with large, clear windows, suggesting seating for a pilot and passengers. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The overall design is typical of general aviation aircraft used for personal or small business travel.
description: A small, white, single-engine low-wing airplane with a tricycle landing gear and a streamlined, enclosed cockpit. Red and gray stripes and a visible registration number accent the fuselage.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full fuselage, low-mounted wings, propeller at the nose, and registration number on the tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, nose wheel, and symmetrical wing span.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with visible cockpit windows, wing, and tail details, including the registration number.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail assembly, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the main landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets; slight dihedral angle present.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with large side windows, likely configured for two or more seats.
